spec_match.rationale: A simulated recording app responds to `interrupted` by stopping the recording, surfacing a banner, and auto-resuming on focus return — exactly the producer-app contract the spec calls out (`exclusive audio access (VoIP) or when a laptop lid is closed`).

interactive.rationale: Six listeners on a real start/stop/interrupt flow with state UI for `recording`, `interrupted by system`, and `user-paused`. Working app shape, not a code card.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Index promises a screen-share app that distinguishes system-interrupted from user-paused with auto-resume; the page implements that distinction visually with separate banners and state tracking.

edge_cases.rationale: Covers the two main states well but doesn't show what happens if an interruption arrives during the saving-progress step (data loss path), nor the case where the system interrupts twice in a row before focus has returned.

references.rationale: `see also` now cites `https://chromestatus.com/feature/5172068166139904` plus the Web Audio API `AudioContextState` spec section.

design_system.rationale: 78 CSS variable references, no raw hex, no button-color mismatch — design-system clean.

openQuestions.title: Show the interrupted-during-save edge case
openQuestions.detail: If an interruption fires while the app is in the middle of saving progress, the demo should walk through whether the save completes, gets cancelled, or is queued. That is the real cliff edge for production recorders.

openQuestions.title: Cover repeated interruptions before focus return
openQuestions.detail: Add a button that fires two interruptions in quick succession — second arriving while still interrupted. The current state machine should be idempotent there, but it's worth demonstrating explicitly so the reader knows the resume strategy doesn't double-fire.

openQuestions.title: Hook to MediaRecorder for realism
openQuestions.detail: Currently a `simulated` recorder. Wiring it to a real `MediaRecorder` with the mic stream (with a clear `mic permission` banner) would convert the page from `mockup` to `here's a working snippet you can drop in`.

summary: Right-shaped app demo — distinguishes system-interrupted from user-paused and ships the auto-resume strategy, with the required ChromeStatus/Web Audio references now present. It would still benefit from explicit save-interrupt/repeated-interrupt branches and a real MediaRecorder path.
